sections.content:
OK, so DOWN moved the marker down! The marker was at lines 19-20 (top portion) and now it's at lines 21-22 (bottom portion). So:
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the marker horizontally along the bar
- UP/DOWN moves the marker vertically within the bar
The marker is a 2x4 block (2 rows x 4 columns) that moves around on the gray bar.
Let me now understand the full structure:
- The gray bar spans roughly rows 19-22, cols 17-41 (4 rows x ~25 columns)
- The marker is 2 rows x 4 columns
- The marker can be at different positions within the bar
- The marker has different color states:
  - `bbSS` (blue border + sky blue fill)
  - `SSbb` (sky blue + blue border)
  - `bbbb` (all blue)
  - `SSSS` (all sky blue)
Wait, looking at the data again:
- After UP: marker at lines 19-20, color `bbbb`
- After DOWN: marker at lines 21-22, color `bbbb` (but the top portion became `SSSS`)
Actually, I think the bar has TWO marker positions - top and bottom. When I pressed UP, it moved the marker to the top half and changed its color. When I pressed DOWN, it moved it to the bottom half.
Let me re-examine. Looking at the before/after:
Before DOWN:
- Lines 19-20: `bbbb` (marker at top)
- Lines 21-22: `SSSS` (something at bottom)
After DOWN:
- Lines 19-20: `SSSS` 
- Lines 21-22: `bbbb` (marker at bottom)
So it seems like there are two "slots" on the bar (top and bottom) and the marker moves between them. The inactive slot shows `SSSS` and the active slot shows `bbbb`.
Actually wait, I need to reconsider. Let me look at the initial state:
Initial lines 19-20: `bbSS` at the right end
Initial lines 21-22: `bbSS` at the right end
So initially both top and bottom had `bbSS`. After my sequence of actions (RIGHT, RIGHT, LEFT, UP, DOWN), the state has changed.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the bar represents two independent cursors or the same cursor with two states.
Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me reset to the initial state and explore more systematically. But I can't reset. Let me instead try to understand the relationship between the bar and the bottom panels.
The bottom-left panel has a purple cross pattern. The bottom-right panel has a checkerboard. Maybe I need to:
1. Position the cursor on the bar using arrow keys
2. The cursor position corresponds to a cell in the 3x3 grid
3. Click or press some key to "select" that cell
Let me try pressing MOUSE on the bar itself to see if that does something.
Actually, let me first try to map out the bar positions. The gray bar seems to have discrete positions. Let me count how many positions there are.
Looking at the initial state:
```
bSSSSggggggggggggggggggggggbbSS
```
The gray bar (g) spans from about col 17 to col 41 (25 cells). The marker (bbSS) is 4 cells wide. So there are about 25/4 ≈ 6 positions horizontally. But the bar might have more fine-grained positions.
Let me try to systematically explore by moving the cursor to different positions and seeing what happens. But first, let me understand the relationship between the bar and the 3x3 grid.
Actually, I realize I should look at this from a different angle. The target is ~36 actions. If the 3x3 grid has 9 cells, and I need to do 4 actions per cell (move to position + select), that's 36 actions. That fits!
Let me try to understand: maybe the bar cursor position maps to grid cells, and I need to visit each cell in a certain order based on the purple pattern.
Let me try pressing MOUSE on one of the grid cells to see if that interacts with the cursor.
Wait, actually, I just realized something. MOUSE didn't work at all before. Let me re-check. The first MOUSE action I tried (on the white tile) resulted in `board_changed: false`. Maybe MOUSE only works on certain elements.
Let me try a different approach. Let me see if I can figure out the bar's position system by trying more LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN actions and tracking the changes.
